name: Route of the Corrals (PR-ZA 3)
name_original: Ruta de los Corrales — circular desde Abejera (PR-ZA03)
description: Hike the moderate 9.2-mile Route of the Corrales circular from Abejera (PR-ZA03) through varied landscapes in Sierra de la Culebra.
region_intro: Hiking around Riofrío De Aliste offers routes through the Río Frío Valley, characterized by riverine landscapes and traditional mills. The region is situated on the southern slope of the Sierra de la Culebra, providing varied terrain. Hikers can encounter features like dry-stone walls ("cortinas") and stone enclosures ("corrales") used historically for livestock. The area provides a mix of gentle pathways along the river and more challenging sections in the sierra foothills. Best hiking trails around Riofrío De Aliste The most popular hiking route is Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rabanales Trail, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.2 km) path. It features gentle pathways and views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Route of the Corrals (PR-ZA 3), a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through areas with traditional stone enclosures, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Riofrío De Aliste is defined by tranquil river valleys, traditional mills, and the foothills of the Sierra de la Culebra. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 7 hikers have used komoot to explore Riofrío De Aliste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Hikes in Riofrío De Aliste feature diverse terrain. You'll find gentle pathways with minimal elevation along the Río Frío Valley, characterized by riverine landscapes. As you venture towards the Sierra de la Culebra, the terrain becomes more varied and can include moderate elevation changes. You'll also encounter unique features like dry-stone walls ("cortinas") and traditional stone enclosures ("corrales") scattered across the landscape.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Yes, there are easy trails available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejera Shoeing Frame loop from Abejera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.3 km route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed walk. The paths along the Río Frío are generally gentle and accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while hiking in the area?
A: While hiking, you can discover several unique features. The region is known for its numerous old traditional mills along the Río Frío, offering a glimpse into local history. You might also encounter ancient dovecotes and the distinctive "cortinas" (dry-stone walls) and "corrales" (stone enclosures) used historically by shepherds. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aliste River in Vegalatrave&lt;/a&gt;. For historical interest, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Valdemera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025181993/castros-de-castillan-y-valdemera-circular-desde-ferreras-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 8.4 km circular trail that offers varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejera Shoeing Frame loop from Abejera&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy circular walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: The region is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Spring brings blooming flora, and autumn offers beautiful colors. Summer can be warm, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended then. Winters are cooler but can still be suitable for hiking, depending on the weather conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Yes, the trails along the Río Frío are particularly suitable for families due to their low difficulty and minimal elevation changes. These paths offer a gentle outdoor activity amidst cultural heritage, with opportunities to see old mills and traditional buildings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejera Shoeing Frame loop from Abejera&lt;/a&gt; is also an easy option for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Most hiking trails in natural areas like Riofrío De Aliste are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, and always clean up after them.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Riofrío De Aliste?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejera Shoeing Frame loop from Abejera&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 25 minutes. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025150633/rabanales-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabanales Trail&lt;/a&gt; (11.2 km), typically take about 3 hours. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejera Shoeing Frame – Corrales de Tozalfreno loop from Abejera&lt;/a&gt; (18.6 km) can take over 5 hours.

Q: What makes the Aliste region, where Riofrío De Aliste is located, unique for hiking?
A: The Aliste region is unique for its blend of serene riverine landscapes, the historical charm of its traditional mills, and its ethnographic structures like "cortinas" and "corrales." Its location on the southern slope of the Sierra de la Culebra provides a diverse backdrop, offering both gentle riverside walks and more extensive exploration into varied terrain, allowing hikers to experience both nature and history.

Q: Are there any particularly scenic routes that offer good views?
A: While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every route, the region's position on the southern slope of the Sierra de la Culebra suggests that higher elevation trails will offer panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025181993/castros-de-castillan-y-valdemera-circular-desde-ferreras-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, with its moderate elevation changes, is likely to provide scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the 'Route of the Corrals' and what can I expect on it?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021973796/route-of-the-corrals-pr-za-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Corrals (PR-ZA 3)&lt;/a&gt; is a 14.8 km trail that leads through areas featuring traditional stone enclosures known as "corrales." These rudimentary circular, oval, or square stone structures were historically used by shepherds to protect livestock. This route offers a unique opportunity to explore the region's ethnographic heritage alongside its natural beauty, typically taking around 4 hours to complete.
tips: Located in the small town of Riofrío de Aliste, we find this beautiful Las Mayadicas Recreational Area. An ideal place to rest and/or recover energy. There are tables and barbecue for picnic. And you can see the Sierra de la Culebra in the background
Nice recreational area where there is a refuge and the Corrales de Tozalfreno, one of these rehabilitated, whose use was to protect cattle from wolves.

From Abejera, heading north to ascend the Sierra de Sesnandez, we come across this Potro de Herrar, a structure that is used to hold horses, donkeys or even cows to make it easier for them to be shoeed or treated. They can be seen in any of the rural towns of the Spanish geography.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Route of the Corrales (PR-ZA03)?
faqs.answer: The Route of the Corrales (PR-ZA03) is generally considered to have low difficulty, making it accessible for a wide range of hikers. While komoot classifies it as moderate, its gentle elevation changes and well-defined path make it suitable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied landscapes, including areas with broom (jara) scrub, chestnut trees, and cultivated fields. You'll also pass under railway tracks via an underpass. The path is generally well-suited for both hiking and mountain biking.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Route of the Corrales?
faqs.answer: The trail covers a distance of approximately 15.5 kilometers (9.6 miles) and typically takes around 3 hours and 40 minutes to complete at a steady pace.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover several interesting spots. These include the Las Mayadicas Recreation Area, the historic Corrales de Tozalfreno, and the unique Abejera Shoeing Frame.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Corrales (PR-ZA03) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Route of the Corrales is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields or near livestock, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: This route is recommended for enjoyment throughout the entire year. Each season offers a different charm, from spring blooms to autumn colors. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de la Culebra?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of the Corrales (PR-ZA03) or to access this part of the Sierra de la Culebra. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Abejera?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Abejera is the starting point for this circular route. You should be able to find public parking within the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of the route overlap with parts of the Camino de las Devesinas. This means you might encounter other hikers or cyclists enjoying these intersecting paths.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Corrales suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its low difficulty and varied scenery, the Route of the Corrales is well-suited for families. The circular nature and moderate length make it an enjoyable outing for children who are accustomed to walking.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Abejera?
faqs.answer: Abejera is a small village, and while specific establishments are not listed, it's advisable to check locally upon arrival for any available cafes or restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike.
